title: Minimum Number of Squares
hints: Think about what the minimum number of squares needed for each number from 1 to A.
For any number i, you can represent it as i = (i - j*j) + j*j for some j where j*j <= i.
Use dynamic programming: dp[i] = minimum number of squares needed to represent i.
Base case: dp[0] = 0 (no squares needed to represent 0).
For each i, try all possible squares j*j <= i and take the minimum: dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i - j*j] + 1).
